name: Textile Engineering
description: Textile Engineering applies engineering principles to the design, manufacturing, and finishing of fibers, yarns, fabrics, and textile products. It covers the entire textile value chain from raw fiber to finished garments and technical textiles.

subjects.name: Textile Fibers
subjects.description: Study of natural and man-made fibers including cotton, wool, silk, polyester, nylon, and acrylic. Covers fiber morphology, physical and chemical properties, and fiber identification techniques.
learning_outcomes: Classify textile fibers based on origin, structure, and properties
Analyze fiber morphology using microscopic techniques
Evaluate physical and chemical properties of major fiber types
Identify unknown fiber samples using standard test methods
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational knowledge for textile manufacturing, sourcing, and quality control across the global textile industry.

subjects.name: Yarn Technology
subjects.description: Covers yarn manufacturing processes including spinning, twisting, and winding for both staple and filament yarns. Includes ring spinning, rotor spinning, air-jet spinning, and fancy yarn production.
learning_outcomes: Design yarn manufacturing processes for different fiber types
Optimize spinning parameters for yarn quality and productivity
Compare spinning technologies for various end-use applications
Evaluate yarn properties including count, twist, and strength
subjects.industry_relevance: Core competency for spinning mills producing yarn for apparel, home textiles, and industrial applications.

subjects.name: Fabric Manufacturing
subjects.description: Study of woven, knitted, and nonwoven fabric production methods. Covers weaving mechanisms, loom types, knitting technologies, fabric geometry, and fabric defect analysis.
learning_outcomes: Design woven fabric structures using weave notation systems
Program knitting machines for various fabric constructions
Troubleshoot fabric defects and optimize loom settings
Compare woven, knitted, and nonwoven fabric properties
subjects.industry_relevance: Core manufacturing process for all textile products from apparel to upholstery to industrial fabrics.

subjects.name: Textile Chemistry
subjects.description: Study of chemical principles applied to textile processing including fiber chemistry, dyeing theory, and chemical finishing. Covers polymer chemistry, dye-fiber interactions, and chemical auxiliaries used in textile processing.
learning_outcomes: Explain chemical structure and reactivity of textile polymers
Analyze dye-fiber bonding mechanisms for different dye classes
Formulate chemical auxiliaries for textile wet processing
Apply chemical principles to troubleshoot processing issues
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for wet processing industries including dyeing, printing, and finishing of textiles.

subjects.name: Textile Testing
subjects.description: Covers textile testing methods for fibers, yarns, fabrics, and garments including tensile strength, abrasion, pilling, colorfastness, and dimensional stability. Includes statistical analysis and international testing standards.
learning_outcomes: Conduct physical and mechanical tests on textile materials
Evaluate colorfastness to light, washing, and rubbing
Apply statistical methods for test data interpretation
Comply with ASTM, ISO, and AATCC testing standards
subjects.industry_relevance: Quality assurance testing is mandatory for textile exports and compliance with international buyer specifications.

subjects.name: Garment Technology
subjects.description: Study of garment manufacturing processes including pattern making, cutting, sewing, finishing, and quality control. Covers production planning, sewing machines, and automation in garment factories.
learning_outcomes: Develop patterns and grading for garment sizes
Design garment production lines for optimized efficiency
Select appropriate sewing machines and handling equipment
Implement quality control systems for garment manufacturing
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for the global apparel manufacturing industry worth hundreds of billions of dollars.

subjects.name: Technical Textiles
subjects.description: Study of technical textile applications including automotive textiles, medical textiles, geotextiles, protective textiles, and smart textiles. Covers performance requirements, testing, and product development for technical applications.
learning_outcomes: Classify technical textiles by application sector
Design textile structures for specific technical performance requirements
Evaluate protective textile performance against heat, chemicals, and ballistic threats
Develop smart textile products integrating sensors and conductive yarns
subjects.industry_relevance: Fastest growing segment of the textile industry with applications in automotive, healthcare, and construction sectors.

subjects.name: Textile Design
subjects.description: Covers principles of textile design including color theory, pattern repeat systems, fabric structure design, and CAD for textiles. Includes jacquard and dobby design, print design, and trend forecasting.
learning_outcomes: Create woven fabric designs using jacquard and dobby mechanisms
Design print patterns with proper repeat systems
Apply color theory principles in textile product development
Use CAD software for textile design and visualization
subjects.industry_relevance: Drives aesthetic differentiation and branding in the competitive textile and fashion markets.

subjects.name: Dyeing & Printing
subjects.description: Study of dyeing and printing processes for textiles including batch and continuous dyeing, screen printing, digital printing, and transfer printing. Covers dye selection, process optimization, and quality evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Design dyeing recipes for different fiber-dye combinations
Optimize dyeing parameters for levelness and reproducibility
Select printing methods based on fabric type and production volume
Evaluate color matching and fastness properties of dyed fabrics
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential value-addition process in the textile supply chain for colored and printed fabrics.

subjects.name: Textile Finishing
subjects.description: Covers mechanical and chemical finishing processes for textiles including sanforizing, calendering, mercerizing, and functional finishes. Includes flame retardant, water repellent, antimicrobial, and easy-care finishes.
learning_outcomes: Design mechanical finishing processes for fabric hand and appearance
Apply functional chemical finishes for specific performance requirements
Optimize finishing formulations for durability and fabric properties
Evaluate environmental compliance of finishing processes
subjects.industry_relevance: Finishing adds significant value to textiles and is crucial for meeting functional and aesthetic market demands.

subjects.name: Nonwovens
subjects.description: Study of nonwoven fabric production including dry-laid, wet-laid, melt-blown, and spunbond processes. Covers web formation, bonding techniques, and applications of nonwovens in hygiene, medical, and filtration sectors.
learning_outcomes: Design nonwoven fabric production lines for various applications
Compare web formation and bonding technologies
Optimize process parameters for nonwoven fabric properties
Develop nonwoven products for hygiene, medical, and filtration markets
subjects.industry_relevance: Nonwovens are one of the fastest growing textile sectors driven by hygiene, medical, and filtration demand.

subjects.name: Textile Composites
subjects.description: Study of textile-reinforced composite materials including 2D and 3D fabric reinforcements, preforming, resin infusion, and composite characterization. Covers applications in aerospace, automotive, and wind energy.
learning_outcomes: Design 2D and 3D fabric reinforcements for composite applications
Select appropriate matrix systems for textile composites
Apply resin transfer molding and vacuum infusion techniques
Evaluate mechanical properties of textile-reinforced composites
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand for lightweight composites in aerospace, automotive, and renewable energy sectors.

subjects.name: Fashion Technology
subjects.description: Covers the intersection of textile technology and fashion including apparel design, fashion forecasting, collection planning, and supply chain management. Includes digital fashion, 3D prototyping, and sustainable fashion practices.
learning_outcomes: Develop fashion collections integrating textile innovations
Apply trend forecasting methodologies for seasonal planning
Use 3D prototyping software for virtual garment sampling
Implement sustainable practices in fashion product development
subjects.industry_relevance: The fashion industry is a major global economic driver requiring technology integration for speed and sustainability.

subjects.name: Textile Machinery
subjects.description: Study of textile machinery design, maintenance, and automation including spinning, weaving, knitting, and finishing machines. Covers machine drives, control systems, preventive maintenance, and modern automation trends.
learning_outcomes: Analyze textile machine mechanisms and drive systems
Design preventive maintenance schedules for textile machinery
Implement automation and PLC control in textile processes
Evaluate energy efficiency and productivity of textile machines
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for textile machinery manufacturers and mill engineering departments for efficient production operations.

subjects.name: Quality Control in Textiles
subjects.description: Covers statistical quality control, inspection systems, and quality management in textile manufacturing. Includes Six Sigma, ISO 9001, total quality management, and textile-specific quality parameters.
learning_outcomes: Apply statistical quality control tools in textile manufacturing
Design inspection systems for in-process and final product quality
Implement Six Sigma projects for defect reduction
Develop quality management systems compliant with ISO standards
subjects.industry_relevance: Quality control is critical for textile exports and meeting demanding specifications from global brands.

subjects.name: Textile Waste Management
subjects.description: Study of textile waste management including waste minimization, recycling, and circular economy principles. Covers textile effluent treatment, solid waste management, and sustainable textile production practices.
learning_outcomes: Design textile effluent treatment plants for regulatory compliance
Develop recycling processes for pre-consumer and post-consumer textile waste
Implement waste minimization strategies in textile production
Evaluate lifecycle impacts of textile products and processes
subjects.industry_relevance: Increasing regulatory pressure and consumer demand for sustainability drive textile waste management expertise.
